senses.definition: an instrument for writing or drawing with ink, usually made of plastic or metal

examples.x: Can you lend me your red pen to fill out this form?
He writes his thoughts and ideas in a journal with a fancy pen.
She uses a black pen to sign important documents.
They keep a collection of colorful pens for drawing and doodling.
We sign our names with a pen when writing greeting cards.

senses.definition: a tool with a slim piece of wood and a thin, colored part in the middle, that we use for writing or drawing

examples.x: He uses a pencil to shade and create different tones in his artwork.
I use a pencil to sketch and draw.
She lends her pencil to a classmate who forgot to bring one.
She sharpens her pencil before starting her math homework.
We mark important passages in a book with a pencil underline.

senses.definition: a type of pen that has a thick tip

examples.x: I keep a set of markers in my art supplies for various art projects.
I use a black marker to write my name on my belongings.
They use washable markers for coloring books and crafts.

senses.definition: a writing instrument filled with ink, typically alcohol-based, designed for writing on whiteboards, erasable surfaces commonly used in classrooms and offices

examples.x: The teacher used a whiteboard marker to write important notes during the lecture.
During the meeting, Mark used a whiteboard marker to brainstorm ideas on the whiteboard.
Sarah purchased a set of colorful whiteboard markers for her home office to keep track of tasks and appointments.
The presenter used a red whiteboard marker to highlight key points on the presentation slides.
Emily accidentally left the whiteboard marker uncapped, causing it to dry out quickly.

senses.definition: a writing tool filled with fast-drying, waterproof ink, typically used for marking on surfaces such as paper, cardboard, plastic, metal, and fabric
a writing instrument filled with pigmented paint or ink, typically used for creating permanent marks on various surfaces such as paper, metal, glass, plastic, wood, and ceramics

examples.x: The artist used a paint marker to add fine details and highlights to their canvas painting.
During the DIY project, Mark used a paint marker to label electrical components for easy identification.
Sarah used a paint marker to customize her skateboard with colorful designs and patterns.
The sign maker used a paint marker to create bold and vibrant lettering on a storefront window.
Emily used a paint marker to decorate a ceramic mug with personalized artwork as a gift.

senses.definition: a special pen filled with ink that has unique properties, like glowing under UV light, used to mark items for security or proof of authenticity
a writing tool filled with liquid chalk, used for drawing or writing on surfaces like chalkboards or glass, and can be easily wiped off with a damp cloth

examples.x: The teacher used a chalk marker to write announcements on the classroom chalkboard.
During the restaurant's daily specials update, the server used a chalk marker to write the menu on the chalkboard.
Sarah used a chalk marker to decorate her storefront window with seasonal greetings.
The event planner used chalk markers to write seating arrangements on glass panels at the wedding venue.
Emily used a chalk marker to create a festive welcome sign on the front door for her housewarming party.

senses.definition: a white or colored stick similar to chalk that is used for drawing or writing
a type of chalk that comes in various hues, used for writing or drawing on chalkboards or other surfaces, commonly used in education, art, or decorative applications

examples.x: The art teacher provided students with colored chalk to create vibrant drawings on the sidewalk during the school's outdoor art event.
During the presentation, the speaker used colored chalk to emphasize key points on the chalkboard, making the information visually engaging.
Sarah used colored chalk to decorate the walls for her daughter's birthday party, adding colorful designs and messages.
Emily enjoyed using colored chalk for her sidewalk chalk art, creating intricate and colorful designs that brightened the neighborhood.

senses.definition: a type of pen with a colored tip, used for marking words in a piece of writing, book, magazine, etc.

examples.x: She used a yellow highlighter to mark important passages in her textbook.
The editor used a pink highlighter to indicate revisions in the document.
Highlighters come in various colors to help categorize information while studying.
He borrowed a green highlighter from his colleague to emphasize key points in his presentation.
The student's notes were filled with highlights in blue and orange from different highlighter pens.

senses.definition: a highlighting tool filled with gel-based ink, used for marking and emphasizing text or passages in documents without smudging or bleeding through the paper

examples.x: During the study session, Sarah used a gel highlighter to mark important information in her textbook without worrying about ink smudges.
The office manager distributed gel highlighters to the team for annotating important sections in the project report without damaging the printed pages.
The student preferred using gel highlighters for color-coding notes in her planner, as the vibrant ink made it easy to organize tasks and deadlines.
Mark reached for a gel highlighter to mark key points in the meeting agenda, ensuring they were addressed during the discussion.
Emily used a gel highlighter to annotate her research articles, appreciating its smooth application and quick-drying ink for efficient studying.

senses.definition: a brand of highlighter pen with soft, translucent ink and a double-ended design, commonly favored by students, artists, and bullet journal enthusiasts
an object that consists of stiff hair or a man-made substitute attached to a handle, used for painting
a specialized writing instrument with a long handle and soft bristles, typically made from animal hair, used for creating elegant and expressive lettering in calligraphy and brush painting
a small tool used for removing the marks of a pencil from a piece of paper

examples.x: He gently rubs the eraser over the words to erase them completely.
He lost his eraser and had to ask his friend to borrow one.
I use an eraser to correct my mistakes when writing.
She shares her eraser with her classmates during exams.
They keep a small eraser in their pencil case for quick corrections.

senses.definition: a tool that erases pencil marks with precision, featuring an eraser core encased in a pencil-like body

examples.x: Sarah used an eraser pencil to neatly remove mistakes from her drawings without smudging the surrounding areas.
During the math test, Mark corrected his answers using an eraser pencil to ensure his work was clear and legible.
The architect used an eraser pencil to refine the lines of her blueprint, making precise adjustments to the design.
Emily preferred using an eraser pencil for sketching outlines, as it allowed her to easily correct and refine her artwork.
The student sharpened her eraser pencil before the exam, knowing it would come in handy for fixing any errors on her paper.

senses.definition: a soft, pliable eraser made of a rubber compound, typically used for removing pencil marks from paper surfaces without damaging the paper
a small tool made of rubber or a similar material used to remove pencil marks from paper

examples.x: She reached for the rubber eraser to correct the mistake in her drawing.
He carefully used the rubber eraser to erase the incorrect equation from the chalkboard.
The students shared a large rubber eraser to clean up their math worksheets.
The artist preferred a soft rubber eraser for delicate shading techniques in her sketches.
He kept a small rubber eraser in his pencil case for quick corrections during exams.

senses.definition: a rectangular tool made of felt or foam used to remove chalk markings from a blackboard

examples.x: The teacher used the blackboard eraser to clean the chalkboard after the lesson.
She wiped the equations off the blackboard with a swift motion of the blackboard eraser.
The students took turns using the blackboard eraser to clear the board before the next class.
He tapped the blackboard eraser against the chalk tray to remove excess dust.
The old blackboard eraser left streaks on the board, so they replaced it with a new one.

senses.definition: a quick-drying fluid typically used to cover mistakes made with pen, ink, or typewriter
a thin strip of white tape used to cover mistakes made when writing or typing

examples.x: Sarah used correction tape to fix the spelling error in her essay.
The office assistant reached for the correction tape to hide the typo in the report.
After making a mistake in his notes, Tom carefully applied correction tape to cover it up.
Emily lent her correction tape to her classmate who had made a mistake in their project.
The student's handwriting became neater after they started using correction tape to fix errors.

senses.definition: a handheld tool with a small blade inside, used for sharpening pencils

examples.x: She used a pencil sharpener to sharpen her dull pencil before the exam.
The artist preferred using a handheld pencil sharpener for sketching on location.
The office manager replaced the old pencil sharpener with a new, more efficient model.

senses.definition: a handheld or desktop device operated by hand to sharpen pencils by rotating them against a blade or abrasive surface

examples.x: In elementary school, we used a small, red manual pencil sharpener attached to the classroom wall.
My grandfather keeps a vintage manual pencil sharpener on his desk, reminding him of his school days.
When camping, we always bring along a portable manual pencil sharpener to keep our pencils ready for sketching in the wilderness.
The teacher handed out manual pencil sharpeners to each student, teaching them the traditional way to maintain their writing tools.
As a collector of office supplies, I have an array of antique manual pencil sharpeners displayed on my shelf, each with its own unique design.

senses.definition: a device that uses electricity to automatically sharpen pencils to a precise point
a specialized tool designed specifically for sharpening the tips of lead or graphite drawing instruments, such as mechanical pencils or drafting leads, to a fine point
a tool used by individuals who are blind or visually impaired to manually create braille characters by embossing dots onto paper or other materials
